mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate over consecutive months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20d forward returns stays below 0.03 across any rolling 2-year window, or returns during declining-rate months are not significantly higher than during flat/rising periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns once the increase exceeds a meaningful threshold.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical instances where UNRATE rises >0.3pp in 3 months, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive, or the average triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth is strong while the fed funds rate is simultaneously high, the market interprets robust demand under tight monetary policy as unsustainable, raising the probability of an eventual demand-driven correction over the following quarter.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with resilient consumer demand
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following joint high-growth/high-rate signals are not significantly negative relative to baseline, or the interaction term's marginal predictive contribution over either series alone is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a sustained deceleration in industrial production growth signals fading supply-side momentum that precedes broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: During identified late-cycle periods, 60-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with INDPRO deceleration (correlation coefficient not distinguishable from zero at 95% confidence).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects ongoing labor demand strength that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue, producing a modest positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive association with monthly PAYEMS growth rate (Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows).

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5%, monetary policy is restrictive enough to meaningfully compress equity valuations and raise recession odds, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns until rates begin easing.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns while FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not significantly below the unconditional historical mean return (t-test p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign in more than one out of three historical restrictive episodes.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing market downturn regime, a sustained decline in housing starts signals broader consumer and construction-sector weakness that transmits to corporate earnings with a lag, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 10% over trailing 6 months
failure_condition: Following HOUST declines >10% in 6 months, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the mean triggered return is not significantly different from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint level of inflation (CPIAUCSL trend) and the federal funds rate determines the real policy rate; when nominal rates fail to keep pace with inflation, real rates turn negative, easing financial conditions and producing short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation rate < 0
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during negative real-rate periods are not significantly higher than during positive real-rate periods (difference-in-means p > 0.10), or the joint interaction term adds no explanatory power beyond FEDFUNDS alone.
